---
title: "When Installments Disagree: A Cross-Path Topology for PPCS Aerobic Documentation"
description: "Six installments each fixed one prescription variable — testing, intensity anchor, weekly dose, staging track, progression logic, monitoring channel. None specifies which combinations of those choices are mutually compatible. This installment audits the interactions directly."
date: "2026-07-09"
series: "Foundation Stack"
installment: pillar2-007
pillar: methods
slug: prescription-path-topology
draft: false
---

::: {.callout-caution}
**Not medical advice. Educational use only. Data from published literature.**
:::

Writing the wearable-monitoring piece, I kept coming back to one line in its own SOP checklist: *document which monitoring channel you used.* Good advice, sized to that piece's problem. It was only after six prior articles were live — what to test, how to anchor intensity without a treadmill, how much to dose, which track a session belongs to, when to advance, what device is allowed to adjudicate — that a less comfortable question showed up. Each SOP had told a clinician what to write down. None of them had said whether the things they were each asking to be written down were allowed to sit on the same page at all.

::: {.callout-note}
## New to this series?

This is **installment 7** of the [Foundation Stack](/index.html) — a PMID-backed methods series on adolescent **PPCS** (prolonged post-concussive symptoms) aerobic exercise prescription. If this is your first visit, start at the [Series Hub](/index.html) or skim the [decision tree](#fig-2-topology) below; the table summarizes what the six prior pieces each established. This page audits how those pieces combine — not a standalone clinical protocol.

**Abbreviations used here:** **BCTT** = Buffalo Concussion Treadmill Test; **RTL** / **RTP** = return to learn / return to sport (consensus documents often label sport steps **RTS**); **CISG** = Concussion in Sport Group consensus staging.

**What the six prior pieces each fixed (this page audits how they combine):**

| Topic | Plain-language scope |
|-------|----------------------|
| [Protocol comparison](/posts/pillar2-001-six-protocols-comparison/) | How hard and how long six published adolescent trials prescribed aerobic exercise |
| [No-treadmill prescribing](/posts/pillar2-002-no-bctt-prescription-paths/) | Fallback intensity anchors when BCTT is unavailable — age-predicted %HRmax, symptom cap, RPE band |
| [Weekly volume](/posts/pillar2-003-twenty-minutes-from-where/) | Whether “20 minutes” means per session, per week, or cumulative dose |
| [School vs sport staging](/posts/pillar2-004-school-vs-sport-staging/) | Separate stepwise checklists for return-to-learn (classes) and return-to-sport (play) |
| [Progression after first Rx](/posts/pillar2-005-progression-after-first-prescription/) | Three advancement rules — weekly BCTT retest (Logic A), CISG symptom-gated steps (Logic B), session-count ramps (Logic C) |
| [Wearable monitoring](/posts/pillar2-006-wearable-hr-error-margins/) | Five “paths” that couple an intensity anchor with a device class — clinic BCTT, home chest strap, wrist optical, symptom cap, or RPE |
:::

#### 1. Problem

The six prior pieces in this series each fixed exactly one prescription variable (see callout table above). Read individually, each closes its own gap. Read together, none states whether a given combination of choices is one a clinician can actually write down and trust.

This article does not propose a new field to fill in. It audits the **interactions already implied by the six prior pieces' own text** — where the series has explicitly declared two choices compatible, where it has explicitly forbidden a combination, and where it has simply never addressed the combination at all. That third category turned out to be the largest, and its size is itself part of the finding: **a flat, all-fields-fillable documentation form would silently imply compatibility the series has never actually claimed.**

The single clearest exception — the one incompatibility this audit found stated outright, twice, in primary text — anchors the rest of the piece:

> "While the RTL and RTS strategies can occur in parallel, student-athletes should complete full RTL before unrestricted RTS." — Amsterdam 2022 [PMID: 37316210]

> "Children and adolescents should not return to sport until they have successfully returned to school." — Berlin 2017 [PMID: 28446457]

The progression piece already names this as a **hard gate**: **Logic A** (raise the ceiling only after a weekly BCTT shows a higher threshold) and **Logic C** (advance because the next session in a fixed ramp was completed) **cannot** check off return-to-sport steps 4–6 (team drills, contact, unrestricted play) while return-to-learn is still below step 4 (full school day without concussion accommodations). This article adds: it is not just a gate on progression *logic* — it is a gate on **which path/logic combinations are eligible to appear on the same documentation bundle at all** once RTP steps 4–6 are in play.

**What this article is not:** it does not run a new trial, and it does not invent a validated documentation standard. It maps literature already established across the six prior pieces into an explicit compatibility record, and separates what the series has *stated* from what this audit *infers* — a distinction Section [Methods](#methods-the-interaction-matrix) keeps visually and structurally separate throughout, not just in a footnote.

#### 2. Methods: the interaction matrix {#methods-the-interaction-matrix}

Six decision dimensions recur across the six prior pieces:

| ID | Dimension | What the audit asks |
|----|-----------|---------------------|
| D1 | BCTT/treadmill threshold available at prescription? | Can the clinic run a Buffalo treadmill test this week to set or retest a heart-rate ceiling? |
| D2+D3 | Intensity anchor + monitoring channel (coupled) | What number or rule stops the session — and which device (if any) is allowed to read it? |
| D4 | RTL / RTP staging | Is the athlete on a school-return step, a sport-return step, or both? |
| D5 | Progression logic | What event authorizes the *next* increase — weekly BCTT (A), CISG symptom-gated step (B), or completed session (C)? |
| D6 | Weekly volume framing | Is the prescription written as minutes per session, sessions per week, or a weekly minute total? |

D2 and D3 are treated as one coupled axis, not two: the wearable piece's **five paths** already bundle anchor + device — **Path 1** clinic BCTT sets the target; **Path 2** home %HR band read by chest strap; **Path 3** wrist optical (not for in-band adjudication); **Paths 4–5** symptom cap or RPE with no HR device. The no-treadmill piece's symptom-cap and RPE anchors do not raise a separate monitoring-channel question the way HR-band anchors do.

Every cell in the matrix below is one of four things, and the distinction is load-bearing, not decorative:

- **Stated-compatible** — a prior piece explicitly describes this combination working together.
- **Stated-incompatible** — a prior piece explicitly forbids or bandwidth-argues against this combination.
- **Silent** — no prior piece addresses this combination, in either direction.
- **Inferred tension** — this audit's own inference from combining two separately-stated rules; not a claim any prior piece makes, and never presented with the same visual weight as the first two categories.

##### What the audit found

Two findings carry the piece:

**Finding 1 — the RTL/RTP gate is the sharpest stated-incompatible rule in the series**, confirmed independently against both Amsterdam and Berlin primary text: RTP steps 4–6 cannot proceed while RTL is below step 4, **regardless of whether advancement follows Logic A (weekly BCTT), Logic B (CISG symptom steps), or Logic C (session-count ramp).** [Figure 3](#fig-3-gate) shows the timing asymmetry: sport steps carry a published 24-hour minimum floor; school steps do not — but both tracks share one hard documentation veto at RTP 4–6.

```{=html}
<figure id="fig-3-gate">
<svg viewBox="0 0 680 340" width="100%" style="max-width:38rem;font-family:system-ui,sans-serif;background:#fafafa;border:1px solid #ddd;border-radius:4px">
  <text x="12" y="16" font-size="10" fill="#e65100" font-weight="600">Sport track only: ≥24 h minimum between steps (not on school track)</text>
  <text x="170" y="36" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12" font-weight="600">RTL — school</text>
  <text x="510" y="36" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12" font-weight="600">RTP — sport</text>
  <!-- row baseline: y = 48 + row*44, rows 0-5 -->
  <!-- RTL column x=40 w=200, RTP x=400 w=200 -->
  <!-- Row 0 -->
  <rect x="70" y="48" width="200" height="32" fill="#e8eaf6" stroke="#5c6bc0" rx="3"/><text x="170" y="68" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10">RTL 1 — symptom-limited</text>
  <rect x="400" y="48" width="200" height="32" fill="#e8f5e9" stroke="#43a047" rx="3"/><text x="500" y="68" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10">RTP 1 — light aerobic</text>
  <text x="378" y="68" font-size="9" fill="#e65100">≥24h</text>
  <!-- Row 1 -->
  <rect x="70" y="92" width="200" height="32" fill="#e8eaf6" stroke="#5c6bc0" rx="3"/><text x="170" y="112" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10">RTL 2 — partial day</text>
  <rect x="400" y="92" width="200" height="32" fill="#e8f5e9" stroke="#43a047" rx="3"/><text x="500" y="112" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10">RTP 2 — sport-specific</text>
  <text x="378" y="112" font-size="9" fill="#e65100">≥24h</text>
  <!-- Row 2 -->
  <rect x="70" y="136" width="200" height="32" fill="#e8eaf6" stroke="#5c6bc0" rx="3"/><text x="170" y="156" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10">RTL 3 — reduced load</text>
  <rect x="400" y="136" width="200" height="32" fill="#e8f5e9" stroke="#43a047" rx="3"/><text x="500" y="156" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10">RTP 3 — non-contact drills</text>
  <text x="378" y="156" font-size="9" fill="#e65100">≥24h</text>
  <!-- parallel bracket rows 0-2 -->
  <path d="M 285 56 L 310 56 L 310 168 L 285 168" fill="none" stroke="#78909c" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="5 3"/>
  <path d="M 360 56 L 385 56 L 385 168 L 360 168" fill="none" stroke="#78909c" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="5 3"/>
  <text x="335" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#546e7a">may run</text>
  <text x="335" y="132" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#546e7a">in parallel</text>
  <!-- Row 3 — RTL 4 threshold -->
  <rect x="70" y="180" width="200" height="32" fill="#c5cae9" stroke="#3949ab" stroke-width="2.5" rx="3"/><text x="170" y="200" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="600">RTL 4 — full day, no accom. ●</text>
  <rect x="400" y="180" width="200" height="32" fill="#ffcdd2" stroke="#c62828" stroke-width="2" rx="3"/><text x="500" y="200" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="600">RTP 4 — team drills</text>
  <text x="378" y="200" font-size="9" fill="#e65100">≥24h</text>
  <!-- Row 4 -->
  <rect x="70" y="224" width="200" height="32" fill="#e8eaf6" stroke="#5c6bc0" rx="3"/><text x="170" y="244" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10">RTL 5 — progressing return</text>
  <rect x="400" y="224" width="200" height="32" fill="#ffcdd2" stroke="#c62828" stroke-width="2" rx="3"/><text x="500" y="244" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="600">RTP 5 — contact</text>
  <!-- Row 5 -->
  <rect x="70" y="268" width="200" height="32" fill="#e8eaf6" stroke="#5c6bc0" rx="3"/><text x="170" y="288" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10">RTL 6 — full return</text>
  <rect x="400" y="268" width="200" height="32" fill="#ffcdd2" stroke="#c62828" stroke-width="2" rx="3"/><text x="500" y="288" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="600">RTP 6 — unrestricted play</text>
  <!-- Gate: red band on RTP 4-6 + condition arrow from RTL 4 -->
  <rect x="392" y="176" width="216" height="132" fill="none" stroke="#c62828" stroke-width="2" stroke-dasharray="6 4" rx="6"/>
  <text x="500" y="318" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" fill="#b71c1c" font-weight="600">SI — hard gate: RTP 4–6 only if RTL ≥ 4</text>
  <path d="M 272 196 L 392 196" fill="none" stroke="#c62828" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arrow)"/>
  <text x="332" y="188" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#b71c1c">threshold</text>
  <defs><marker id="arrow" markerWidth="8" markerHeight="8" refX="6" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M0,0 L6,3 L0,6 Z" fill="#c62828"/></marker></defs>
  <text x="12" y="332" font-size="10" fill="#444">Weekly BCTT retest, CISG steps, and session ramps cannot override this veto. Gate is documentation eligibility, not a ban on all same-day activity.</text>
</svg>
<figcaption><strong>Figure 3 — Dual-track gate (RTL × RTP).</strong> Schematic dual-track ladder (not a recovery timeline). <strong>Left:</strong> return-to-learn (RTL) steps — symptom-limited attendance through full school day without concussion accommodations. <strong>Right:</strong> return-to-sport (RTP) steps — consensus tables label sport steps RTS; this series uses RTP in clinical prose. Orange <strong>≥24 h</strong> marks the published minimum between <em>sport</em> steps only (Amsterdam + Berlin). RTL steps have no equivalent published floor. <strong>Red RTP boxes 4–6</strong> = stated-incompatible while RTL is still below step 4 (team drills / contact / unrestricted play before full school day). ● = gate quote located in both consensus documents (37316210, 28446457).</figcaption>
</figure>
```

**Reading Figure 3.** Tracks may run in parallel at lower steps (Amsterdam: RTL and RTS strategies can occur in parallel). The incompatibility is specific: **high-contact / unrestricted sport steps** (RTP 4–6) while **RTL is still below step 4** — not a ban on all simultaneous activity.

**Finding 2 — most cross-installment combinations are silent, not incompatible.** Of **24 audited cells** in the two priority triangles (19 in Figure 1a + 5 in the dual-track grid), **14 are silent (58%)**, **6 stated-incompatible (25%)**, and **4 stated-compatible (17%)** — see [Figure 1c](#fig-1c). No prior piece states whether, for example, a session-ramp progression (Logic C) is an appropriate way to advance a symptom-cap-primary prescription (Path 4 — stop on symptom rise, no HR device). Silence is not permission. A flat form that lets a clinician fill every box would imply validated compatibility the literature does not support — the same failure mode as treating a wrist watch as if it were a chest strap, but one layer up in the paperwork.

**Paths (intensity + monitoring, read before Figure 1a).** **Path 1** = clinic BCTT sets the HR ceiling; **Path 2** = home %HR band adjudicated by chest strap; **Path 3** = wrist optical (ancillary only — cannot trust narrow HR bands); **Path 4** = symptom cap; **Path 5** = RPE band. **Logic A** = advance after weekly BCTT (or session-tolerant stage ladder variants); **Logic B** = advance CISG RTL/RTP steps when symptoms stay below the gate; **Logic C** = advance because the next session in a fixed ramp was completed (Micay +5%/session or Kurowski duration titration). [Figure 2](#fig-2-topology) shows how paths bundle with logics.

```{=html}
<figure id="fig-1a">
<svg viewBox="0 0 770 210" width="100%" style="max-width:44rem;font-family:system-ui,sans-serif;background:#fafafa;border:1px solid #ddd;border-radius:4px">
  <!-- grid: col_x = 100 + i*60, i=0..10; row0 y=35 h=42; row1 y=77 h=42 -->
  <!-- column headers -->
  <text x="130" y="25" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" font-weight="600">P1×A</text>
  <text x="190" y="25" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" font-weight="600">P1×B</text>
  <text x="250" y="25" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" font-weight="600">P1×C</text>
  <text x="310" y="25" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" font-weight="600">P2×A</text>
  <text x="370" y="25" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" font-weight="600">P2×B</text>
  <text x="430" y="25" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" font-weight="600">P2×C</text>
  <text x="490" y="25" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" font-weight="600">P3×A</text>
  <text x="550" y="25" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" font-weight="600">P3×C</text>
  <text x="610" y="25" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" font-weight="600">P4×B</text>
  <text x="670" y="25" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" font-weight="600">P4×C</text>
  <text x="730" y="25" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" font-weight="600">P5×C</text>
  <!-- row labels -->
  <text x="50" y="60" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11">BCTT yes</text>
  <text x="50" y="102" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11">BCTT no</text>
  <!-- row0: BCTT yes -->
  <rect x="100" y="35" width="60" height="42" fill="#2e7d32"/><text x="130" y="60" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12" fill="white" font-weight="600">SC</text><circle cx="152" cy="41" r="5" fill="white" stroke="#1b5e20" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <rect x="160" y="35" width="60" height="42" fill="#e0e0e0"/><text x="190" y="60" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12" fill="#333">SL</text>
  <rect x="220" y="35" width="60" height="42" fill="#e0e0e0"/><text x="250" y="60" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12" fill="#333">SL</text>
  <rect x="280" y="35" width="60" height="42" fill="#e0e0e0"/><text x="310" y="60" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12" fill="#333">SL</text>
  <rect x="340" y="35" width="60" height="42" fill="#e0e0e0"/><text x="370" y="60" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12" fill="#333">SL</text>
  <rect x="400" y="35" width="60" height="42" fill="#e0e0e0"/><text x="430" y="60" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12" fill="#333">SL</text>
  <rect x="460" y="35" width="60" height="42" fill="#c62828"/><text x="490" y="60" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12" fill="white" font-weight="600">SI</text><circle cx="512" cy="41" r="5" fill="none" stroke="white" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="512" y="44" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="white">S</text>
  <rect x="520" y="35" width="60" height="42" fill="#c62828"/><text x="550" y="60" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12" fill="white" font-weight="600">SI</text><circle cx="542" cy="41" r="5" fill="none" stroke="white" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="542" y="44" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="white">S</text>
  <rect x="580" y="35" width="60" height="42" fill="#e0e0e0"/><text x="610" y="60" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12" fill="#333">SL</text>
  <rect x="640" y="35" width="60" height="42" fill="#e0e0e0"/><text x="670" y="60" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12" fill="#333">SL</text>
  <rect x="700" y="35" width="60" height="42" fill="#e0e0e0"/><text x="730" y="60" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12" fill="#333">SL</text>
  <!-- row1: BCTT no -->
  <rect x="100" y="77" width="60" height="42" fill="white" stroke="#bbb" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="130" y="102" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12" fill="#999">—</text>
  <rect x="160" y="77" width="60" height="42" fill="white" stroke="#bbb" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="190" y="102" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12" fill="#999">—</text>
  <rect x="220" y="77" width="60" height="42" fill="white" stroke="#bbb" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="250" y="102" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12" fill="#999">—</text>
  <rect x="280" y="77" width="60" height="42" fill="#e0e0e0"/><text x="310" y="102" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12" fill="#333">SL</text>
  <rect x="340" y="77" width="60" height="42" fill="#e0e0e0"/><text x="370" y="102" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12" fill="#333">SL</text>
  <rect x="400" y="77" width="60" height="42" fill="#e0e0e0"/><text x="430" y="102" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12" fill="#333">SL</text>
  <rect x="460" y="77" width="60" height="42" fill="#c62828"/><text x="490" y="102" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12" fill="white" font-weight="600">SI</text><circle cx="512" cy="83" r="5" fill="none" stroke="white" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="512" y="86" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="white">S</text>
  <rect x="520" y="77" width="60" height="42" fill="#c62828"/><text x="550" y="102" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12" fill="white" font-weight="600">SI</text><circle cx="542" cy="83" r="5" fill="none" stroke="white" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="542" y="86" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="white">S</text>
  <rect x="580" y="77" width="60" height="42" fill="#2e7d32"/><text x="610" y="102" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12" fill="white" font-weight="600">SC</text><circle cx="632" cy="83" r="5" fill="white" stroke="#1b5e20" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <rect x="640" y="77" width="60" height="42" fill="#2e7d32"/><text x="670" y="102" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12" fill="white" font-weight="600">SC</text><circle cx="692" cy="83" r="5" fill="white" stroke="#1b5e20" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <rect x="700" y="77" width="60" height="42" fill="#2e7d32"/><text x="730" y="102" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12" fill="white" font-weight="600">SC</text><circle cx="752" cy="83" r="5" fill="white" stroke="#1b5e20" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <!-- legend -->
  <rect x="100" y="140" width="16" height="16" fill="#2e7d32"/><text x="122" y="152" font-size="11">stated-compatible</text>
  <rect x="280" y="140" width="16" height="16" fill="#c62828"/><text x="302" y="152" font-size="11">stated-incompatible</text>
  <rect x="470" y="140" width="16" height="16" fill="#e0e0e0"/><text x="492" y="152" font-size="11">silent</text>
  <rect x="560" y="140" width="16" height="16" fill="white" stroke="#bbb" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="582" y="152" font-size="11">pruned (n/a)</text>
  <circle cx="108" cy="178" r="5" fill="white" stroke="#333" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="122" y="182" font-size="11">● primary source</text>
  <circle cx="248" cy="178" r="5" fill="none" stroke="#333" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="254" y="181" font-size="9">S</text><text x="268" y="182" font-size="11">◎ synthesis</text>
</svg>
<figcaption><strong>Figure 1a — Triangle 1 grid (D1 × Path 1–5 × Logic).</strong> Stated-compatible / stated-incompatible / silent only — no inferred-tension cells share this color language (see Figure 1b). "Pruned" cells are category mismatches (Path 1 presupposes BCTT), not stated incompatibilities. <strong>Badges:</strong> ● = located in primary text; ◎ = synthesis from two verified wearable-piece claims — wrist optical error band wider than prescription band blocks in-band adjudication under any logic that depends on home HR-zone completion.</figcaption>
</figure>
```

```{=html}
<figure id="fig-1c">
<svg viewBox="0 0 520 120" width="100%" style="max-width:32rem;font-family:system-ui,sans-serif;background:#fafafa;border:1px solid #ddd;border-radius:4px">
  <text x="10" y="22" font-size="12" font-weight="600">24 audited cells</text>
  <rect x="10" y="34" width="87" height="28" fill="#2e7d32"/><text x="53" y="52" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" fill="white">SC 17%</text>
  <rect x="97" y="34" width="130" height="28" fill="#c62828"/><text x="162" y="52" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" fill="white">SI 25%</text>
  <rect x="227" y="34" width="303" height="28" fill="#e0e0e0"/><text x="378" y="52" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" fill="#333">SL 58% (14 cells)</text>
  <text x="10" y="88" font-size="11" fill="#444">Triangle 1 alone: 11/19 silent (58%). Triangle 2 (dual-track): 3/5 silent, 2/5 SI (RTP 4–6 gate).</text>
</svg>
<figcaption><strong>Figure 1c — Audited cell inventory.</strong> Counts from the two priority triangles (Figure 1a: 19 non-pruned cells; dual-track grid: 5 cells). Silent cells dominate — the finding is the <em>absence</em> of cross-piece statements, not a scatter of incompatibilities.</figcaption>
</figure>
```

```{=html}
<figure id="fig-1b">
<svg viewBox="0 0 770 150" width="100%" style="max-width:44rem;font-family:system-ui,sans-serif;background:#fafafa;border:1px solid #ddd;border-radius:4px">
  <rect x="10" y="10" width="750" height="55" fill="#fff8ec" stroke="#c9971f" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="6 3" rx="4"/>
  <text x="22" y="28" font-size="12" font-weight="600" fill="#7a5b00">T-001 — Logic C × Path 3 (wrist, ancillary-only)</text>
  <text x="22" y="45" font-size="11" fill="#444">Logic C advances when "the next session was completed." If zone membership was read from a wrist sensor whose error band is wider than the prescription band, that completion is not verifiable. No prior piece states this pairing directly.</text>
  <rect x="10" y="78" width="750" height="55" fill="#fff8ec" stroke="#c9971f" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="6 3" rx="4"/>
  <text x="22" y="96" font-size="12" font-weight="600" fill="#7a5b00">T-002 — Same-day RTL+RTP combined symptom load, any logic</text>
  <text x="22" y="113" font-size="11" fill="#444">Proposed default: if either school or sport track shows &gt;2/10 symptom rise for &gt;1 hour on a day, hold both tracks the next day — explicitly labeled unpublished in the progression piece. Adopted convention, not a finding.</text>
</svg>
<figcaption><strong>Figure 1b — Tension register (inferred, not stated).</strong> Dashed border, no fill color — deliberately not styled like Figure 1a's stated cells, so neither looks like a citation of the other.</figcaption>
</figure>
```

##### Figure 2 — Decision topology, pruned {#fig-2-topology}

```{mermaid}
flowchart TD
  A{BCTT available at prescription?}
  A -->|Yes| P1["Path 1<br/>BCTT sets threshold (clinic)"]
  P1 --> P2["Path 2<br/>home chest-strap, %HR band"]
  P2 --> LA["Logic A<br/>weekly BCTT retest"]
  A -->|No| P45["Path 4/5<br/>symptom cap or RPE, no device"]
  P45 --> LB["Logic B<br/>CISG step rules"]
  P45 --> LC["Logic C<br/>session ramp / duration (Kurowski, Micay)"]
  A -.->|either| P3["Path 3<br/>wrist, ancillary only"]
  P3 -.-> DEAD["DEAD — R(wrist) > 1<br/>blocks in-band adjudication under Logic A or C"]

  classDef dead fill:#fdecea,stroke:#c62828,stroke-dasharray: 4 3,color:#7a1f1f;
  class P3,DEAD dead;
```

**Reading Figure 2.** Solid arrows are stated-compatible bundles from Table A; the dashed arrow into Path 3 marks wrist optical as stated-incompatible for in-band HR adjudication under Logic A or C. The P1→P2 arrow traces the Leddy-trial sequence (clinic BCTT, then home Polar strap) — **not** an exclusive dependency: when no treadmill exists, **non-BCTT anchors** (age-predicted %HRmax, symptom cap, or RPE from the no-treadmill prescribing piece) can still pair with a home chest strap without ever passing through Path 1. Kurowski's Pittsburgh program titrates bike-session **duration** at clinic visits (Logic C), not BCTT retest — so Path 1 × Logic C stays silent by design. **Not shown as a tree branch:** the RTL/RTP cross-track gate ([Figure 3](#fig-3-gate)) — RTP steps 4–6 cannot be documented while RTL is below step 4, regardless of which bundle above was chosen. That gate applies *after* a bundle is running, not at initial path selection.

#### 3. Comparison: what the primary text specifies {#comparison-table-a}

*Table A — claims independently located in primary text (PMC full text or local PDF). Inferential material appears in [Disagreements](#disagreements), not here.*

**Design key:** **RCT** = randomized trial methods text; **Feasibility RCT** = Micay adolescent SRC feasibility study; **RCT (pilot)** = Kurowski Pittsburgh SE pilot; **Consensus** = CISG consensus document tables (not trial outcomes); **Management protocol** = published clinical management paper (session-tolerant Logic A variant); **Clinical review** = narrative review with explicit prescription algorithm (not trial methods).

| Source | Design | What the primary text specifies | Documentation element | PMID |
|--------|--------|----------------------------------|------------------------|------|
| Leddy/Buffalo RCT | RCT | "a provided Polar H7 Bluetooth Heart Rate Sensor and Fitness Tracker to monitor HR" | Monitoring device class | [30715132](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/30715132/) |
| Leddy/Buffalo RCT | RCT | New target HR set by weekly clinic BCTT for as long as participant remained symptomatic | Progression logic (A) | [30715132](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/30715132/) |
| Leddy 2013 BCTT review | Clinical review | "increase the exercise HR target by 5 to 10 bpm every 2 wk (via phone call or email)"; athletes 10 bpm/2 wk, nonathletes 5 bpm/2–3 wk; optional BCTT repeat every 2 wk for precision | Progression logic (A, non-weekly-BCTT fallback) | [24225521](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/24225521/) |
| Leddy 2021 stage-ladder | Management protocol | Advance on session tolerance; re-evaluate every 1–2 weeks; goal 80%+ HRmax without exacerbation | Progression logic (A, session-observed variant) | [34817719](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34817719/) |
| Kurowski / Pittsburgh SE | RCT (pilot) | Cycling program 5–6 days/week at 80% of the duration that exacerbated symptoms; dose reassessed at each of 6 clinic visits | Progression logic (C, duration-titrated variant) | [27120294](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/27120294/) |
| Micay fixed ramp | Feasibility RCT | 8 fixed sessions; intensity +5% age-predicted HRmax per session until 70%, no clinician adjustment mid-ramp | Progression logic (C, fixed-ramp variant) | [30018795](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/30018795/) |
| Amsterdam 2022 | Consensus | RTS table: "each step typically takes a minimum of 24 hours"; RTL table carries no equivalent floor | Progression logic (B) — asymmetric time floor by track | [37316210](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37316210/) |
| Berlin 2017 | Consensus | RTS: "at least 24 hours (or longer) for each step... go back to the previous step" if symptoms worsen; no parallel sentence on the RTL table | Progression logic (B) — same asymmetry, prior consensus | [28446457](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/28446457/) |
| Amsterdam 2022 | Consensus | "student-athletes should complete full RTL before unrestricted RTS" | Cross-track gate | [37316210](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37316210/) |
| Berlin 2017 | Consensus | "Children and adolescents should not return to sport until they have successfully returned to school" | Cross-track gate | [28446457](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/28446457/) |

[Figure 4](#fig-4-ramps) below visualizes the three intensity-shaped progression models in the Micay, Leddy 2021, and Kurowski rows.

```{=html}
<figure id="fig-4-ramps">
<svg viewBox="0 0 660 200" width="100%" style="max-width:42rem;font-family:system-ui,sans-serif;background:#fafafa;border:1px solid #ddd;border-radius:4px">
  <!-- Panel A — Micay -->
  <text x="110" y="18" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" font-weight="600">A · Micay (Logic C)</text>
  <text x="110" y="30" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#666">%HRmax · 8 sessions</text>
  <line x1="30" y1="155" x2="200" y2="155" stroke="#999"/>
  <line x1="30" y1="155" x2="30" y2="45" stroke="#999"/>
  <text x="18" y="105" font-size="8" fill="#666" transform="rotate(-90 18 105)">%HRmax</text>
  <text x="115" y="172" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#666">session</text>
  <text x="26" y="153" font-size="8" fill="#666">50</text>
  <text x="26" y="68" font-size="8" fill="#666">70</text>
  <polyline points="40,125 65,113 90,101 115,89 140,77 165,77 190,77 215,77" fill="none" stroke="#1565c0" stroke-width="2"/>
  <circle cx="40" cy="125" r="3" fill="#1565c0"/><circle cx="140" cy="77" r="3" fill="#1565c0"/>
  <text x="145" y="72" font-size="8" fill="#1565c0">cap 70%</text>
  <!-- Panel B — Leddy 2021 -->
  <text x="330" y="18" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" font-weight="600">B · Leddy 2021 (Logic A)</text>
  <text x="330" y="30" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#666">%HRmax · symptom-tolerant steps</text>
  <line x1="250" y1="155" x2="420" y2="155" stroke="#999"/>
  <line x1="250" y1="155" x2="250" y2="45" stroke="#999"/>
  <text x="246" y="153" font-size="8" fill="#666">50</text>
  <text x="246" y="58" font-size="8" fill="#666">80</text>
  <polyline points="260,125 290,113 320,101 350,89 380,77 410,65 440,53" fill="none" stroke="#6a1b9a" stroke-width="2" stroke-dasharray="4 2"/>
  <circle cx="260" cy="125" r="3" fill="#6a1b9a"/><circle cx="440" cy="53" r="3" fill="#6a1b9a"/>
  <text x="400" y="48" font-size="8" fill="#6a1b9a">goal ≥80%</text>
  <text x="330" y="172" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#666">stage (hold if +2 symptoms)</text>
  <!-- Panel C — Kurowski -->
  <text x="550" y="18" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" font-weight="600">C · Kurowski (Logic C)</text>
  <text x="550" y="30" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#666">duration index · 6 visits</text>
  <line x1="470" y1="155" x2="640" y2="155" stroke="#999"/>
  <line x1="470" y1="155" x2="470" y2="45" stroke="#999"/>
  <text x="466" y="153" font-size="8" fill="#666">80</text>
  <text x="466" y="68" font-size="8" fill="#666">100</text>
  <polyline points="490,77 530,77 530,70 570,70 570,63 610,63 610,56 650,56" fill="none" stroke="#2e7d32" stroke-width="2"/>
  <circle cx="490" cy="77" r="3" fill="#2e7d32"/>
  <text x="495" y="88" font-size="8" fill="#2e7d32">80% of threshold</text>
  <text x="550" y="172" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#666">clinic visit (illustrative)</text>
  <text x="330" y="192" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#888">Schematic shapes from Table A quotes — not fitted patient data.</text>
</svg>
<figcaption><strong>Figure 4 — Progression logic ramp shapes (Table A).</strong> Small-multiples of the three <strong>intensity-shaped</strong> progression models located in Table A (not head-to-head comparable outcomes). <strong>A:</strong> Micay fixed %HR<sub>max</sub> ramp (+5%/session, cap 70%, 8 sessions). <strong>B:</strong> Leddy 2021 stage-ladder (%HR<sub>max</sub> steps on session tolerance; hold if symptoms +2; goal ≥80%). <strong>C:</strong> Kurowski duration titration (home dose = 80% of clinic symptom threshold duration; reassessed at 6 visits — <em>y-axis is duration index, not HR</em>). Logic B (CISG step rules) is omitted: sport-step cadence is not a %HR prescription curve. Buffalo weekly-BCTT Logic A is retest-gated, not a fixed ramp — also omitted.</figcaption>
</figure>
```

#### 4. Disagreements {#disagreements}

The disagreements below are not a head-to-head debate between named camps — the six prior pieces mostly do not talk to each other on these specific questions. They are the audit's silent and inferred-tension cells, presented as prose rather than as table rows.

**Disagreement 1 — does progression logic interact with monitoring channel at all?** The progression piece defines three advancement rules without saying which device reads the heart-rate band; the wearable piece argues a wrist-optical channel cannot adjudicate a narrow band regardless of intensity anchor. No prior piece states whether Logic C's mechanism — advance because "the next session was completed" — depends on the session actually having been confirmed in-band. If that confirmation came from a wrist reading with reported disagreement wider than the prescribed band, "completed in zone" is a soft claim, not a documented one. This is this audit's own inference (Tension register T-001), not a finding either source states, and it is not presented with the same weight as the two gate quotes in Table A.

**Disagreement 2 — is the same-day combined-load rule a finding or a convention?** The progression piece proposes that a symptom escalation on either the school or sport track should hold advancement on both tracks the next day — and states in the same passage that "this rule has no published source." Any documentation format this series produces will need that rule somewhere. It should appear labeled exactly as that piece labeled it: an adopted clinical-judgment default, not a citation (Tension register T-002).

**Disagreement 3 — is silence permission?** Of the **24 audited cells** in the two priority triangles, **14 (58%) are silent** — no prior piece addresses that combination in either direction ([Figure 1c](#fig-1c)). That is the largest category, not a residual one. A documentation format that lets a clinician check any box in any combination treats that silence as if it had been resolved in the combination's favor. The literature has not resolved it either way. **What would settle this disagreement?** A published protocol that states, for a given progression logic and monitoring channel pairing, whether the combination was deliberately chosen or merely co-occurred in one trial's design — a distinction none of the six source articles currently makes explicit.

#### 5. SOP: before you write the note {#sop-documentation-grammar}

*This is a **methods** SOP — documentation grammar, not a printable clinic form. The one-page prescription note and path-picker tool are reserved for the paired pillar4 installment; link there when live.*

**Step 1 — Declare the bundle, not the fields.** Before any line is written, record two labels the progression and wearable pieces use but rarely appear together on the same sheet:

| Label | Allowed values | What each value means |
|-------|----------------|------------------------|
| **Path** | 1–5 | **1** clinic BCTT sets HR ceiling · **2** home %HR band read by chest strap · **3** wrist optical (ancillary only) · **4** symptom cap · **5** RPE band |
| **Logic** | A · B · C | **A** weekly BCTT retest (or session-tolerant stage ladder) · **B** CISG RTL/RTP symptom-gated step · **C** next session/visit in a fixed ramp completed |

If the pair is **stated-incompatible** in the interaction matrix (e.g., Path 3 used to adjudicate narrow HR-band membership), do not proceed — change Path or change the control variable (see wearable piece bandwidth rules).

**Step 2 — Record shared context (same meaning on every path).**

| Field | What to write |
|-------|----------------|
| RTL step / RTP step | Current step on each ladder: **RTL** = return-to-learn (symptom-limited class attendance → full day without concussion accommodations); **RTP** = return-to-sport (light aerobic → unrestricted play) |
| Logic label | A, B, or C — which advancement rule is active |
| Reassessment | Date + responsible clinician/AT |

**Step 3 — Apply the cross-track gate before RTP steps 4–6.** Table A rows for Amsterdam and Berlin are not background reading — they are a **hard documentation veto**: if RTL is below step 4 (full school day without accommodations), RTP steps 4–6 (team drills, contact, unrestricted play) cannot be checked off, **regardless of Logic A, B, or C**. A form that allows both is worse than no form.

**Step 4 — Fork path-specific items inside the module, not in a shared header.**

| Topic | Why it forks |
|-------|----------------|
| Same-day combined-load hold | Operational definition differs when the session stop rule is HR-band vs symptom-cap — progression piece proposes holding both tracks the next day if either track shows >2/10 symptom rise for >1 hour; label as **adopted convention**, not Table A finding (see Disagreement 2) |
| Device class / HR ceiling | Only for Paths 1–3; Paths 4–5 use symptom/RPE primacy |

**Step 5 — Flag silent combinations.** If the chosen Path × Logic × staging × weekly-volume pairing has **no** stated-compatible or stated-incompatible cell in the matrix, write **"combination not addressed in series sources"** on the note — do not leave the boxes filled as if validated.

**Wrist + HR-band adjudication:** **Path 3 is stated-incompatible with using a wrist readout to adjudicate narrow HR-band membership under any Logic that depends on in-band completion** — not Logic C alone. Logic A's weekly BCTT retest does not remove the bandwidth problem for home sessions read from a wrist sensor.

::: {.callout-tip}
## Printable note (pillar4)

When published, the path-locked one-page prescription note and the `prescription-record` tool (planned in pillar4-001) will implement Steps 1–5 in layout (mutually exclusive modules). Until then, use this section as the documentation grammar only.
:::

#### 6. Limitations

1. **Hypothesized gap, not field-tested.** The problem statement — that six prior pieces do not compose into one mutually compatible record — is an internal audit diagnosis. No clinic usability study or reader survey has validated that clinicians experience this as a barrier.

2. **Matrix scope.** Two priority triangles (D1×Path×Logic; D4×Logic×D6) do not exhaust every pairwise interaction across six dimensions. Remaining pairs are backlog or genuinely silent.

3. **Triangle 2 scoped to dual-track.** The school-vs-sport staging piece defines two parallel step ladders — **return-to-learn (RTL)**: symptom-limited attendance through full school day without concussion accommodations; **return-to-sport (RTP)**: light aerobic work through unrestricted play. The progression piece applies advancement rules across both ladders at once, including the cross-track gate that blocks sport steps 4–6 until RTL reaches step 4. This matrix audits RTL × RTP × Logic combinations only when **both tracks are active**. Athletes recovering on a single track (e.g., a non-athlete with no sport ladder) are not given separate matrix rows here — an editorial prune to keep the grid finite, not a claim that single-track care does not exist.

4. **Inferred tensions are not evidence.** T-001 and T-002 (Disagreements 1–2) are audit inferences or the progression piece's explicitly unpublished defaults. They belong in documentation design discourse, not in Table A.

5. **No new trial comparisons.** This article does not claim one Path × Logic bundle produces better outcomes — only whether the series' own text permits writing the combination down without contradiction.
